programmes.show_description: H from Steps has your ultimate party playlist. Not just for Fridays!
See programme schedules for BBC Radio 5 Live.
Everything you need to know as you wake up.
Your dedications and song requests from the 1950s-90s.  Can you guess the Stuck in the Middle song? Text 81012, email moneyfornothing@bbc.co.uk or call 03700 100 110.
Hollywood star Kiefer Sutherland chats to Lucy about bringing his music to Cardiff in May, and Dr Sian Williams on her new book, The Power of Anxiety.
All the day's Welsh football and live commentary from The Six Nations: Wales v Italy. Kick-off 4.40pm.
Bethan shares Immersed sessions with Martha Elen from Y Felinheli and INGRET from Ukraine, recorded by Horizons. Plus new Welsh releases in The Wales Mix.
A chat with Huw Marc Bennett, the best new Welsh music, and archive from Andrew Falkous.
Relax, unwind and chill out with an hour of classic and contemporary tunes.
See programme schedules for BBC Radio 5 Live.
Lucy chats with Katherine Jenkins about the books that matter to her and her children. Welsh writer Olivia Wakeford talks about her debut novel, My Dog.

programmes.show_name: We Can't Go On Eating Like This!
programmes.show_description: Farmers are protesting, but what's at the root of their anger, and how does it relate to the challenges we face on climate, nature and the basic task of feeding Wales?
Megan Williams visits an award-winning arable farm near St Ishmaels in Pembrokeshire. We also hear how new legislation aims to better protect livestock from dog attacks.
A service led by Revd Dr Jennie Hurd, a district chair of the Methodist Church in Wales.
Nick Servini with political news, discussions and analysis, plus a review of the Sunday papers.
Azim Ahmed hosts a panel discussion with a live audience at BBC Wales in Cardiff Central Square to mark Ramadan 2026.
A second dose of Owen Money for the weekend. It's non-stop music all the way , with  classics from the 60s , 70s and 80s and your dedications!
Robin brings you the best in music and guests for your Sunday afternoon. Call 03700 100 110 Text 81012 or message us via X @bbcradiowales.
Music, entertainment, and great guests for your Sunday afternoon.
Roy hears from author Dr Sean Cunningham about the Welsh links of Henry V11. And accents - are they thriving or in decline? Dr Sarah Cooper from Bangor University joins Roy.
After the news crews leave, what happens next? Catrin Nye presents a series about the aftermath and the long term effects of tragedy and trauma on humans and on a community.
A service led by Revd Dr Jennie Hurd, a district chair of the Methodist Church in Wales.
Frank Hennessy presents the best in folk, roots and acoustic music from the Celtic countries and beyond.
The finest musical melodies, exquisite performances from stage and screen, plus orchestral and choral works from Wales and beyond. No need to book your seat, just listen and relax.
